items.briefing:
CrowdStrike has warned that cybercriminals are actively targeting popular AI coding tools with malware, which is relevant to any staff using AI assistants for work tasks. Separately, a security flaw has been discovered in Ubuntu Linux that could allow attackers to gain full control of a system — if your organisation uses Ubuntu, ensure IT applies available patches promptly.

On a positive note, KnowBe4 has launched an AI-powered security awareness video tool specifically for Australian and New Zealand organisations, which could support staff training efforts here at the RTO.

**Tip:** If you use any AI tools for work, only download them from official, verified sources and keep them updated.

featured.title: Ubuntu snap-confine flaw can give attackers root access
featured.quote: Ubuntu vulnerability allows local attackers root access on default desktop installations.
